settings.error.envMustBeObject: Environment variables must be a JSON object
settings.title: Settings
settings.roles.title: Agent Roles & Tools
settings.roles.basicTools.label: Basic toolkit (read / write / search / python / shell / structured_patch)
settings.roles.basicTools.desc: Inject the basic toolkit into every built-in role, so Leader does not have to re-dispatch when an agent (e.g. ux_designer) lacks file_create
settings.roles.restartHint: Reloaded into Leader; running agents keep their snapshot, new dispatches use the new definition
settings.roles.agent.name: Name
settings.roles.agent.tools: Tools (comma-separated; empty uses default worker tools)
settings.roles.agent.gitIdentityHint: Override git author identity for this role's commits
settings.roles.agent.confirmDeleteMessag: This deletes the {{scope}} definition file for agent {{role}}. Running agents are unchanged; new dispatches will stop using it.
settings.roles.addToolDesc: Choose from the live tool catalog and tools already known by other roles. This writes a role override.
settings.roles.noToolsToAdd: No known tools are available to add.
settings.roles.restoreDefaultsDesc: Clear this role's added/removed tool overrides and restore the preset toolkit.
settings.roles.alreadyDefault: This role is already using the default toolkit
settings.roles.confirmRemoveMessage: This will remove {{tool}} from role {{role}}. You can restore it from Removed tools or use Restore defaults.
settings.roles.confirmResetMessage: This will clear every added and removed tool override for role {{role}} and restore the preset toolkit.
settings.langfuse.enabled.desc: When enabled, LLM calls and agent activities will be reported to the Langfuse observability platform. Disabled by default, does not affect any existing functionality.
settings.langfuse.baseUrl.desc: Use https://cloud.langfuse.com for Langfuse Cloud, or your self-hosted deployment URL
settings.langfuse.secretKey.desc: Obtain from Langfuse project settings, starts with sk-lf-
settings.langfuse.publicKey.desc: Obtain from Langfuse project settings, starts with pk-lf-
settings.langfuse.connectionTest.desc: Verify that the Langfuse server is reachable
settings.langfuse.traceLlmCalls.desc: Generate a generation span for each LLM call, recording model, input/output, and token usage
settings.langfuse.traceToolCalls.desc: Generate a span for each tool call
settings.langfuse.traceAgentLifecycle.de: Record agent creation, completion, failure and other lifecycle events
settings.langfuse.maskSensitive.desc: Mask keys, tokens and other sensitive information in prompts before reporting
settings.item.model.desc: Model used by the leader agent
settings.item.agentModel.desc: Model used by sub-agents (leave empty to use Leader model)
settings.item.localLlmGateway.desc: Expose OpenAI and Anthropic compatible APIs on the current Web port for local AI app testing.
settings.item.localLlmGatewayProvider.de: Both endpoint formats stay available, but only this provider's env vars are injected.
settings.item.localLlmGatewayModel.desc: Injected as OPENAI_MODEL or ANTHROPIC_MODEL so agents do not waste time probing model lists.
settings.item.localLlmGatewayInjectEnv.d: Inject the local Gateway base URL, API key, and model into terminals, Python, and agent subprocesses.
settings.item.localLlmGatewayOverrideExi: Off by default; user project OPENAI_* / ANTHROPIC_* configuration takes priority.
settings.item.mcpEnabled.desc: Expose the unified MCP tool and installed MCP servers to agents.
settings.item.mcpToolTimeoutMs.desc: Maximum wait time for MCP list, call, and resource requests.
settings.item.userAgent.desc: Identifies Lingxiao on outbound model API requests
settings.item.proxyLlmRequests.desc: Route model API requests through this proxy
settings.item.proxyToolRequests.desc: Use this proxy for tools, browser, screenshots, and Shell/Python child processes
settings.item.externalAgentsEnabled.desc: Allow Claude Code CLI / Codex CLI to run as sub-agents. Enabled by default; roles are registered only when the matching CLI is installed.
settings.externalAgents.missingHint: Missing CLIs will not register their roles. Install them in the environment running Lingxiao before use.
settings.item.provider.desc: LLM provider, affects API format and authentication
settings.item.maxConcurrency.desc: Maximum number of agents that can run in parallel. Changes take effect immediately via hot reload.
settings.item.workerCompletionJudgeEnabl: Off by default. When off, worker completion uses deterministic hard guards only: substantive summary, no latest tool error, and contract compliance proof. When on, Lingxiao adds one model judgment with higher latency and token/API cost.
settings.item.memory.autoMemoryEnabled.d: Automatically save useful information to memory
settings.item.imageHistoryRetainRounds.d: Number of rounds to retain image history
settings.item.toolExecutionTimeoutMs.des: Fallback timeout for tools without their own timeout. Shell, Python, HTTP, and custom tools can declare longer execution windows.
settings.item.env.desc: Set environment variables for the session
settings.item.sandbox.enabled.desc: Run commands in a sandboxed environment
settings.item.sandbox.autoAllowBashIfSan: Automatically allow bash commands when running in sandbox mode
settings.item.permissionMode.desc: Single permission mode for backend, Web UI, TUI, and tool execution. yolo means full access; networked allows allowlisted network; dev/strict require approval.
settings.item.permissionMode.yoloHint: YOLO / Full Access: unrestricted internet and local file access. Use only in trusted local environments.
settings.item.hardenedMode.tagline: For enterprise intranet / multi-user deployments. Off by default.
settings.item.hardenedMode.desc: Lingxiao defaults to single-user local/intranet use, where the operator has full host capabilities. Hardened mode is for enterprise intranet / multi-user deployments: it desensitizes python/terminal env vars, restricts file read/write to the workspace, minimizes the sandbox bind mounts, tightens outbound network access, and enforces stricter rate limits and token checks. Workflows that rely on these capabilities may be affected.
settings.item.hardenedMode.locked: Locked by deployment environment (LINGXIAO_HARDENED_MODE); cannot be turned off here.
settings.item.hardenedMode.confirm.title: Enable hardened mode?
settings.item.hardenedMode.confirm.body: The following capabilities will be tightened: python/terminal env desensitization, file read/write limited to the workspace, minimized sandbox bind, restricted outbound network, and stricter rate limits and token checks. Existing workflows that depend on these may break.
settings.item.envAllowlist.desc: In hardened mode, only these env vars are passed to python_exec / terminal / sandbox child processes (others, including API Key / Token vars, are stripped). One per line or comma-separated; leave empty to use the built-in minimal default set.
settings.item.gitPlatform.desc: Git hosting platform for MR/PR management
settings.git.tokenMaskedHint: Current token: {{token}}. For security reasons, tokens cannot be edited directly in the Web UI.
settings.git.tokenReadOnlyHint: For security reasons, tokens cannot be edited directly in the Web UI. Configure it through the settings file or deployment environment.
settings.item.gitDefaultBranch.desc: Default base branch for new MRs/PRs
settings.item.gitPreCommitGate.desc: Run type check / build before allowing git commit
settings.item.gitPreCommitGateCommand.de: Custom shell command to run before commit (optional)
settings.item.serverRandomPort.desc: Assign a random OS port on each start instead of fixed 8080, reducing detectability
settings.item.intuition.desc: Invisible understanding context. No keyword rules, no fake intent matching.
settings.item.tacitMode.desc: Low-interruption partner mode: understand first, ask only when truly needed.
settings.item.intuitionProfile.desc: How strongly Lingxiao should behave like a long-term autonomous partner.
settings.addModel.name: Name
settings.addModel.maxTokensHint: Maximum tokens for a single model response
settings.addModel.error.required: Model, API key, and base URL are required.
settings.addModel.apiKeyPlaceholderForUp: Leave blank to keep the existing key
settings.model.desc: Configure the model registry, active Leader and Agent models, local gateway injection, and reasoning behavior in one place.
settings.model.activeDesc: Shows how the configured model library flows into Leader, Agent, Gateway, and reasoning settings.
settings.model.selectionDesc: Choose configured model IDs. The server validates these against the model library before saving.
settings.model.libraryDesc: Add or update OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible endpoints. API keys are never echoed back.
settings.model.addModelDesc: Create a named model ID that Lingxiao can use everywhere else.
settings.model.editModelDesc: Leave API key blank to preserve the existing secret.
settings.model.displayNameHint: This is the model ID shown in Lingxiao settings.
settings.model.realModelHint: This is the model name sent to the provider API.
settings.model.emptyLibrary: No custom models configured yet. Add a model endpoint before selecting a Leader model.
settings.model.gatewayDesc: Expose local OpenAI and Anthropic compatible endpoints for projects, terminals, and agent subprocesses.
settings.model.reasoningDesc: Tune thinking effort and whether reasoning content is visible in the UI.
settings.model.deleteInUse: This model is used by Leader, Agent, or Gateway
settings.model.deleteConfirm: Delete model {{name}}? This removes the endpoint and saved secret from the model library.
settings.model.noFilteredModels: No models match the current filters.

wiki.emptyState.desc: Auto-generate structured documentation for your project and track code changes. Give your agents deeper codebase awareness.
wiki.emptyState.needSession: Create a session first to associate a project path

wiki.progress.waitingHint: Live content will appear here during doc generation

eternal.title: Eternal Mode
eternal.status.off.desc: Enable to let Leader continuously decompose, execute, and verify toward your goal without manual intervention
eternal.empty.title: Eternal Mode Off
eternal.empty.desc: When enabled, Lingxiao will keep driving toward your stated goal until there is verifiable delivery
eternal.patrol.title: Run History
eternal.intent.title: Enable Eternal Mode
eternal.intent.subtitle: Tell Lingxiao the goal it should keep completing so autonomous work stays anchored
eternal.intent.focus.label: Persistent Goal
eternal.intent.focus.placeholder: e.g. Keep fixing WebUI/TUI/backend runtime sync bugs and add verification…
eternal.intent.focus.hint: Lingxiao will treat this as the highest-priority goal for decomposition, execution, and acceptance
eternal.intent.priority.label: Highest Priority Task (optional)
eternal.intent.priority.placeholder: e.g. Complete #42 login flow refactor first…
eternal.intent.priority.hint: For urgent tasks, Lingxiao will handle these first
eternal.intent.forbidden.label: Off-limits Files/Dirs (optional)
eternal.intent.forbidden.hint: Lingxiao will not modify any content under these paths
eternal.intent.interval.label: Check-in Cadence
eternal.intent.interval.placeholder: e.g. After each major task, every hour, after 3 tasks…
eternal.intent.skip.hint: After you set a goal, Lingxiao will keep driving toward it. You can still send messages to adjust, pause, or disable Eternal Mode at any time.
command.cost.description: View token usage and cost for current session
command.bughunt.description: Start white-box audit + instrumentation + black-box verification
daemon.title: Daemon Control
daemon.qqbot.title: QQ Bot
daemon.qqbot.startDaemonFirst: Start the daemon first to enable QQ Bot.
settings.item.dangerousCommandGuard.desc: Block destructive commands like rm -rf /, fork bombs, and encoding bypasses. Disabled by default; when enabled, agents are prevented from executing high-risk commands.
settings.item.identityJudgeLlm.desc: Off by default. When off, Lingxiao only uses local rules to block clear system-prompt/hidden-instruction probes. When on, a few ambiguous suspicious requests trigger one extra Leader-model judgment, improving nuance at the cost of first-turn latency and token/API usage.
settings.item.blockPrivateNetwork.desc: Prevent HTTP tools from accessing private network addresses (127.0.0.1, 10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x). Disabled by default; suitable for scenarios requiring network isolation.

canvas.execution.empty: Execution results will appear here after running a node
traces.noDataHint: After running tasks, agent events will appear here
workers.info.description: Active Leader and Agent worker processes. Each session has one Leader for decision-making that can dispatch multiple Agents for subtasks.
workers.info.hint: Click the trash icon to terminate a worker. Daemon mode keeps processes running in the background.
workers.noWorkersHint: After starting a conversation, the Leader process will appear here

design.market.title: Lingxiao System Theme Library
design.market.subtitle: Themes are presented as complete product systems, covering everything from login and onboarding to settings, security, audit, and status feedback.
design.market.noPrompt: No reference prompt provided for this system theme.
office.confirm.compactSlash: Compact the current context? This triggers /compact.
office.confirm.interrupt: This interrupts the current session/run, not just this agent. Continue?
office.placeholder.terminal: e.g. check the current build status
office.placeholder.dispatch: Enter a new task to dispatch...
office.note.terminal: The office has no real terminalId, so this sends to the session for the Leader/Agent to act on terminal-related intent.
office.note.coffee: The coffee machine triggers a real /compact.
office.note.elevator: Does not create a new session directly to avoid missing cwd/prompt context; dispatches a new task to the current session.
office.note.plan: Calls the real session/approvePlan or session/rejectPlan.
office.note.board: Opens the planning area and can request the Leader to summarize the current plan.
office.note.toolLab: Does not fabricate plugin lists; requests a real tool-call summary.
office.note.officeLink: This furniture has no destructive actions; opens the relevant live area panel.
onboarding.welcome.title: Welcome to Lingxiao
onboarding.welcome.desc: Lingxiao is a multi-agent coding assistant. You need to configure an LLM before getting started.

onboarding.workspace.title: Select Workspace
onboarding.workspace.desc: The workspace is the working directory where Lingxiao operates on files.
